title: peer-grill claims dump
description: Schema for <agent>.claims.yaml files. Backwards-compatible: pre-existing dumps without verifier/falsifier fields validate but grade as UNVERIFIABLE.

agent.description: Unique agent identifier across this peer-grill topic.

session_started.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp when this dump began.

id.description: Stable slug. Same id across agents = same subject. Different statement = disagreement, not collision.

statement.description: One sentence asserting what is true.

confidence.description: high=checkable source; medium=inferred but source may be stale; low=no source, expected to lose if challenged.

source.description: How you know — file path+line, command output, signed-off doc, or 'inferred from X'.

fingerprint.description: Greek-alphabet visual fingerprint, AUTO-DERIVED from id via sha256(NFC(id))[:4 bytes] mod 24. Display-layer only; never load-bearing. Empty string if not yet computed. Schema accepts but tools should regenerate from id rather than trust an authored value.

verifier.description: Optional. Runnable command that should pass when the claim is true. Absence => UNVERIFIABLE in grading.

falsifier.description: Optional. Runnable command that demonstrates the claim is FALSE if it succeeds. Absence => no falsification protocol; reviewer must produce one ad-hoc.

disputation.description: Optional. Scholastic quaestio form for high-stakes contested claims. See docs/conventions/dialectic-vocabulary.md.
Quaestio-form disputation per scholastic tradition (Aquinas Summa Theologiae I-I, q.1-q.119). Use only for claims whose stakes earn the structural overhead.

quaestio.description: The thesis being disputed, stated as a question or proposition. (Greek λόγος — the account that must be defensible.)

obiectiones.description: Numbered objections to the quaestio. Each carries a source the asker can verify.

source.description: How the objector knows — file, command, prior decision.

raised_by.description: Agent slug who raised this obiectio.

sed_contra.description: The single strongest counter that pulls toward the right answer. Aquinas reserves this for the most authoritative source — quote it.

source.description: The authoritative source. Highest weight a reasonable peer would accept.

respondeo.description: The author's adjudicating response. Concedes what the obiectiones got right, restores the sed contra. Should be substantive, not dismissive.

responsiones.description: Per-objection replies, each addressing a specific obiectio by number.

to.description: Number of the obiectio this reply addresses.

aletheia_sha256.description: Hex sha256 of the canonical merged statement after RATIFY. ἀλήθεια (truth-as-unconcealment) attestation. Set only after both peers ratify.

cmd.description: Shell command to execute. Must be deterministic enough to be re-runnable from any reviewer's box. Avoid network calls; prefer filesystem + stable APIs.

note.description: Optional human-readable rationale: why this command, what it proves, what it doesn't prove.
